summary: Controller code review allows HARBOR-241 at the reviewed head. The dedicated route performs fixed-format bearer authorization before session lookup, uses timing-safe byte comparison, rejects missing/malformed/duplicate/query/body variants, requires an active user-held managed session with canonical identity/profile/execution bindings, persists before exposing the public state, and returns a redacted public identity fact only. Tests cover authorization, session and identity boundaries, persistence failure, restart readback, and sensitive-value non-disclosure. No automatic login, Cookie/DOM inspection, credential storage, site task, or external write is introduced.
reviewed_validation_summary: 2026-07-10 local validation on `work/harbor-241-manual-auth-sync`: `pnpm install --offline --frozen-lockfile`, `pnpm typecheck`, focused Runtime API tests, `pnpm test` (39 passed), and `git diff --check` passed. Review corrections enforce same-identity session reuse, user-held session control, atomic persistence before public mutation, and public user-confirmed provenance. Fixture tests only; no Cookie, DOM, page content, or live identity state was read or changed.
follow_ups: After Harbor #241 and App #236 merge, perform packaged App E2E against the user-confirmed managed session before closing either user story.

semantic_review_disposition.reason: Controller-authored implementation review found no P0-P2 authorization, identity-binding, persistence, or public-data-boundary defects.
